The Court noted that the stipulation was signed by the Objectors, but not by the Beneficiaries. Mr. Njelita stated that another stipulation was signed by the Beneficiaries including the contingent Beneficiaries on July 19th, that a proposed order was sent in on July 17th, that everyone has agreed to release the money to the Trustee and that this was also stipulated to by Child Support Services. The Petition to Enforce Money Judgment Against Trust Beneficiary is continued to August 6, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Petition Concerning The Internal Affairs Of A Trust And Declaratory Relief/Damages
Off Calendar Apr-02-2018 Continued To Jul-09-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Melvin Hodges for Lamerle Johnson (deceased)
Maxwell Njelita for Jeanell Johnson (Trustee)

The Court noted that Lamonte Johnson, Dushawn Johnson, and Sarah Summerall were not present. Counsel reported that the parties have come to a settlement agreement as of Saturday, March 31, 2018, that the document has been circulated for signatures, Danielle Johnson will sign on behalf of the Estate of Lamerle Johnson, and requested a 90 day continuance for filing of a petition for approval of settlement agreement. Mr. Njelita addressed the issue of splitting the Chase account into multiple banks, stating that it would create more work and costs, and that Judge Stewart was okay with the account remaining as is due to the parties reaching settlement soon. The Petition Concerning Internal Affairs of Trust and for Declaratory Relief Funds on Deposit with Chase Bank is continued to July 9, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Charlene P. Kiesselbach. Clerk: Shirley Le, Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.
